title: Fixing Payment Gateways for Cameroon Digital Products: The Solution Blocked Creators Actually Use — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: Tens of thousands of Cameroon digital creators are blocked from selling their ebooks, courses, and art internationally due to PayPal's and Stripe's country restrictions, and their banks' unwillingness to facilitate online transactions. This limits their earning potential and stifles their creativity. However, there is a solution that has been working for hundreds of creators in restricted countries like Nigeria, Ghana, and Pakistan. It's time to break free from these limitations and start selling digital products globally.
The Real Reason This Problem Exists

The problem of blocked payment gateways is not just a matter of geography or banking regulations. It's a symptom of a larger issue: the lack of infrastructure designed for digital creators in restricted countries. Current solutions like Paystack and Interswitch are either expensive, unreliable, or both. They often require a bank account, which many creators in Cameroon and other countries do not have or are not allowed to use.
Why the Obvious Fixes Do Not Work

The P2P exchange ecosystem in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, and Pakistan has matured significantly alongside crypto adoption. Platforms like Binance P2P, local exchanges, and mobile money integrations allow creators to convert USDT, BTC, and other coins to naira, cedis, Kenyan shillings, and Pakistani rupees at near-market rates. Transactions typically complete within 30 minutes. The conversion fees are significantly lower than what PayPal or Western Union charge on the same corridor. This means the practical outcome of receiving crypto is not that you are holding volatile assets — it is that you are getting paid in a form you can immediately convert to local currency at a competitive rate.
The Infrastructure That Solves It

The solution lies in using blockchain-based payment processors like NOWPayments, which allow creators to receive payments in 300+ cryptocurrencies, without the need for a bank account or KYC. This infrastructure is designed specifically for digital creators in restricted countries and provides a seamless payment experience for buyers and sellers alike. Web3 Wallet Connect, a frontend wallet integration tool, makes it easy to set up a digital product store that accepts cryptocurrency payments from anywhere in the world.
Blockchain-based payment processors eliminate country restrictions and bank account requirements.
How to Implement the Solution Today

To start selling digital products internationally today, Cameroon creators can follow these steps. First, sign up for a NOWPayments account and connect it to their digital product store. Next, set up Web3 Wallet Connect to enable cryptocurrency payments. Finally, create a digital product and list it for sale on their store. With these steps, creators can start earning money from international sales within minutes.
What Success Looks Like After the Fix

After implementing the blockchain-based payment solution, Cameroon creators can expect to increase their earning potential, expand their customer base, and gain greater control over their digital product sales. They can also sell their products without worrying about country restrictions, bank account requirements, or high transaction fees. With this solution, they can focus on creating and selling their digital products, rather than worrying about the payment process.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
Cryptocurrency payments increase earning potential and expand customer bases.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're a creator in Nigeria or Ghana looking for a similar solution, check out our digital product store demo at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html. With our infrastructure, you can start selling digital products globally and earning money from international sales within minutes.
Your Action Checklist

Confirm which coins have the best P2P conversion options in your country before you configure your payment settings — USDT on Tron is a safe starting point for most African and South Asian markets. Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect so buyers can see live prices and QR codes for payment. Run a complete test transaction on mainnet and confirm delivery works end to end. Set up a P2P exchange account — Binance P2P, a local exchange, or both — so you have a conversion route ready the moment your first real sale comes in. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
Digital creators can now focus on creating and selling their products, rather than worrying about the payment process.
